comment.body: Totally agree. But beauty and elegance in itself don't have a value. And if you put it differently: in most cases you don't need a distributed ledger (aka a database). A well set up central database with redundancies will be more energy efficient and easier to handle. You really only need this if you cannot trust the other parties. Which brings you back to criminals, libertarians etc. But for a regular industry usage a blockchain is just a very inefficient tool to handle data storage. Same with the related smart contracts. No reason why this cannot be solved without blockchain
comment.raw_body: Totally agree. But beauty and elegance in itself don't have a value. And if you put it differently: in most cases you don't need a distributed ledger (aka a database).
